summary.description_full:
The strategy expresses tactical exposure to equity volatility based on short term momentum extremes in an inverse volatility ETP. It monitors a 6 period RSI on SVXY (inverse VIX futures exposure) on hourly data and assumes that RSI regime shifts in SVXY proxy short term volatility risk on or off. The intended edge is to capture mean reversion and regime transitions in volatility by switching between long inverse volatility (risk on, short vol) and long volatility (risk off, long vol) when SVXY moves into or out of oversold or overbought conditions.

The portfolio trades only two listed volatility ETPs: SVXY and VXX, using full notional allocation to a single instrument (100 percent) when a signal triggers; otherwise it can be flat. Decisions are evaluated multiple times per day via scheduled intraday checks after the equity open. Entries occur on RSI crossovers: go long SVXY when RSI rises through 70 (momentum/risk on) or rises through 15 (oversold reversal), and go long VXX when RSI falls through 30 (risk off). A risk reduction rule liquidates all positions when RSI falls back below 85 after being above it, effectively taking exposure off after extreme overbought conditions.

raw.name: SVXY RSI Regime Switch Volatility Strategy

author.name: Alex Muci
author.bio:
I develop systematic trading strategies in equity, futures and option markets relying on tools such as Python (Pandas, Pandas, Scikit-learn), SQL, MS Office.
Previously I was a structurer in two investment banks where I created and marketed trading strategies for institutional investors (new products development), and set-up UCITS funds and other retail products. 
PhD in Finance from Imperial College, London.

raw.description:
The strategy expresses tactical exposure to equity volatility based on short term momentum extremes in an inverse volatility ETP. It monitors a 6 period RSI on SVXY (inverse VIX futures exposure) on hourly data and assumes that RSI regime shifts in SVXY proxy short term volatility risk on or off. The portfolio trades only two listed volatility ETPs: SVXY and VXX, using full notional allocation to a single instrument (100 percent) when a signal triggers; otherwise it can be flat. Decisions are evaluated multiple times per day via scheduled intraday checks after the equity open.
